sections.heading: S
sections.section_value: The patient reports intermittent numbness and tingling in both hands for the past few weeks, beginning after a car accident last December. She notes worsening symptoms over the last month and reports mild neck stiffness following the accident. She describes the tingling as most prominent in the right hand, extending from the middle and ring fingers to the forearm, and more pronounced in the left hand with the pinky and ring fingers. She reports occasional throbbing pain, but overall feels weaker, particularly when using her hands. She experiences this primarily at night and throughout the day. She denies any loss of sensation in general, but feels less sensitive than expected. She has not been consistently taking nerve pain medication. The patient reports fatigue and weight gain as recent changes. She also reports excessive appetite and bloating. She notes floaters in her vision and reports shortness of breath and leg swelling. She states she is always hungry.

sections.heading: O
sections.section_value: Physical exam reveals diminished pinprick sensation in the third and fourth fingers of the right hand and the fourth and fifth fingers of the left hand. Reflexes are one plus and symmetric at the upper extremities and one plus and symmetric at the lower extremities. Tinel's signs are positive at both wrists and both elbows. Neurological exam is otherwise unremarkable. Blood pressure is mildly low. Heart rate is within normal limits.
medical_concepts: third and fourth fingers of the right hand
fourth and fifth fingers of the left hand
symmetric reflexes at the upper extremities
symmetric reflexes at the lower extremities
positive Tinel's sign at both wrists
positive Tinel's sign at both elbows

sections.heading: A
sections.section_value: Bilateral carpal tunnel syndrome is suspected. Cubital tunnel syndrome cannot be ruled out. The patient's symptoms are consistent with peripheral neuropathy.

sections.heading: P
sections.section_value: Schedule an EMG and NCV study. If symptoms do not resolve, consider referral to hand clinic for possible carpal tunnel release surgery or symptomatic injections. Encourage consistent splint use. Follow up on EMG/NCV results. Counsel the patient on the importance of adherence to her current medications, including carvedilol, lexapro, and addressing her pre-diabetes and anxiety.

medical_concepts: third and fourth fingers of the right hand
fourth and fifth fingers of the left hand
symmetric reflexes at the upper extremities
symmetric reflexes at the lower extremities
positive Tinel's sign at both wrists
positive Tinel's sign at both elbows
